title: Smoke retro — verifies panel rendering across all heatmap cells and reversal states
honest_assessment:
This retro is a synthetic fixture written by the skill scaffolding. Three findings exercise the full Table-1 ramp (sev-5 reversed cleanly, sev-3 capped because the chain broke, sev-1 trivial). Three wins exercise the Table-2 ramp.

Real retros should describe what actually happened in plain prose, including what surprised the agent and where confidence was wrong.

table_1_root_causes.issue: Backend kept serving old code after Go source edit

whys.statement: Backend served handler from before edit
/tmp/vector-backend binary on disk was new but the running process was old
Launcher supervisor caught SIGTERM and refused to respawn
Supervisor treats SIGTERM as graceful and waits for in-flight requests, but a long-poll held it open
Long-poll timeout default is 5 min and supervisor has no force-kill escalation

reversal.chain: 5-min long-poll default produces a graceful-shutdown stall
graceful-shutdown stall blocks supervisor respawn on SIGTERM
blocked respawn forces the old binary to keep serving
old binary serving produces stale handler responses

resolution_steps: Add SIGKILL escalation in launcher supervisor after 10s SIGTERM grace.
Document the SIGKILL trap in `docs/c_launcher.md` with the symptom signature.
Add a smoke test that asserts respawn on edit-and-rebuild within 12s.

table_1_root_causes.issue: docs/c_launcher.md described an older spawn flow

whys.statement: Reader followed steps that no longer matched the code
The doc was written before the supervisor refactor
No doc-sync gate was triggered when the supervisor changed
The author marked the change as 'internal refactor, no doc impact' which correlates with most internal refactors

reversal.chain: self-classification correlates with skipping doc review
skipped doc review produces stale documentation
stale doc produces wrong reader steps

resolution_steps: Tighten doc-sync trigger to fire on any change in `cmd/launcher/**` or `internal/launcher/**`.
Replace the 'no doc impact' self-classification with a positive checkbox listing impacted docs.

table_1_root_causes.issue: Heatmap cells originally written with inline style props

whys.statement: Cells used style={{ background: ... }} on first draft
Author reached for the fastest expression of the idea

resolution_steps: Lift to catalog classes ui-retro__cell--h1..h5 / --w1..w5 in globals.css.

table_2_what_went_well.win: Mirroring /research's accordion + JSON-on-disk pattern saved an entire UI design pass.
table_2_what_went_well.why_it_worked: Reusing a proven shape kept scope tight and avoided an Opus-tier architecture decision.

table_2_what_went_well.win: Loop-detector hook caught a spec gap (no user-msg reset) before it shipped.
table_2_what_went_well.why_it_worked: Writing the injector hook second made the missing handshake obvious.

table_2_what_went_well.win: Held to the no-inline-style rule even under time pressure.
table_2_what_went_well.why_it_worked: The CSS HARD RULE is loud enough that it interrupts shortcuts.

table_2_what_went_well.win: Used the existing Backlog board format rather than inventing a new column scheme.
Sub-tab default = Ledger so recurring patterns surface first.
table_2_what_went_well.why_it_worked: Matches the actual user need: 'what keeps biting me?' over 'what happened in retro N?'.
